key_insight: LLM persona has two layers: aggregate trait scores stay mostly stable, while the deeper geometric structure of responses changes sharply with question framing.
engineering_relevance: Persona evaluation systems for LLMs should be frame-aware, because ordering and context can hide or distort the geometry that aggregate scores miss.
method: Built within-instance correlation matrices from IPIP-50 questionnaire responses.
Compared geometry on SPD manifolds under manipulated question orderings.
Tested GPT-4o persona behavior for American and Chinese-American frames.
evidence_signal: Under randomization, aggregated Big Five features dropped 21%, geometric features collapsed 42% under frame misalignment, and geometry recovered to 84% under shared frames versus 76% for aggregates.
watch_item: The frame-dependent geometry may not transfer cleanly across prompts, models, or cultural persona settings without careful calibration.
paper_title: The Dual Nature of LLM Persona: Aggregated Tendencies and Frame-Dependent Geometry
